steps.reaction_equation: trans-Cinnamate + NADPH + O2 + H+ --> 4-Coumarate + NADP+ + H2O
p-Coumaroyl-CoA + 3 Malonyl-CoA + 3 H+ --> Naringenin chalcone + 4 CoA + 3 CO2
Naringenin + NADPH + O2 + H+ --> 2-Hydroxy-2,3-dihydrogenistein + NADP+ + H2O

heterologous_genes.function: Phenylalanine ammonia-lyase; deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamate + NH4+
heterologous_genes.expression_notes: Soluble cytoplasmic protein. High Vmax (10.5 umol/sec/mg). Km=64 uM suitable for yeast intracellular Phe levels. GRAVY -0.171 (hydrophilic), instability index 33.5 (stable). No cofactor requirements.

known_mutations.effect: ref3-3; reduces lignin deposition, dwarf phenotype

heterologous_genes.expression_notes: CYP450 enzyme requiring co-expression with NADPH-cytochrome P450 reductase (ATR1/CPR). ER membrane-anchored via N-terminal TM helix. Requires heme cofactor. Instability index 42.8 (borderline unstable). STRING interactions: strong association with PAL1/PAL2/CHS/4CL1 (scores >0.99). AlphaFold model available (AF-P92994-F1). Consider N-terminal truncation and replacement with yeast ER signal if expression is poor.

Soluble protein. Requires Mg2+ cofactor (abundant in yeast). Highly specific for 4-coumarate (Km=38 uM) over cinnamate (Km=6320 uM). Crystal structure available (PDB: 3TSY) for rational design. Instability index 49.2 (potentially unstable); consider codon optimization.

heterologous_genes.function: Chalcone synthase; condenses coumaroyl-CoA with 3 malonyl-CoA to naringenin chalcone
heterologous_genes.expression_notes: Soluble cytoplasmic protein. Multiple crystal structures available. Consumes 3 malonyl-CoA per reaction - malonyl-CoA supply is the likely bottleneck for CHS flux. Active site Cys169 critical for catalysis.

heterologous_genes.function: Chalcone--flavanone isomerase; cyclizes naringenin chalcone to (2S)-naringenin
heterologous_genes.expression_notes: Small, soluble, stable protein (instability index 34.2). Legume-origin (Medicago) chosen for compatibility with downstream isoflavonoid enzymes. 9 PDB structures available. Low expression risk. Selected from alfalfa because it can also process 6'-deoxychalcone to liquiritigenin, providing flexibility.

heterologous_genes.function: 2-hydroxyisoflavanone synthase (isoflavone synthase); converts naringenin to 2-hydroxy-2,3-dihydrogenistein via aryl migration
heterologous_genes.expression_notes: CYP450 enzyme (CYP93C family) requiring co-expression with CPR. ER membrane-anchored via N-terminal TM helix. Requires heme cofactor. This is the KEY bottleneck enzyme as it catalyzes the unique aryl migration reaction (B-ring migration from C2 to C3 position) defining isoflavonoids. STRING interactions: strong association with HIDH (score 0.978), CHI1A (0.939), CHS8 (0.869). AlphaFold model available (AF-Q9SWR5-F1). Consider multi-copy integration due to expected low activity. Instability index 42.2 (borderline).

known_mutations.effect: Reduction of both dehydratase and carboxylesterase activities
Reduction of both dehydratase and carboxylesterase activities
Reduction of both dehydratase and carboxylesterase activities
Complete loss of both dehydratase and carboxylesterase activities
Complete loss of both dehydratase and carboxylesterase activities

heterologous_genes.expression_notes: Soluble protein from soybean. Alpha/beta hydrolase fold. Active site triad: T164 (proton acceptor), D263, H295 (proton donor/acceptor). Co-express with IFS2 from same organism for metabolon compatibility. STRING analysis shows HIDH is the top interaction partner of IFS2 (score 0.978), suggesting physical association. Note: the 2-hydroxyisoflavanone intermediate is unstable and can spontaneously dehydrate, but HIDH greatly accelerates and improves stereospecificity.

heterologous_genes.function: Isoflavone 4'-O-methyltransferase; methylates genistein at 4'-OH to produce biochanin A using SAM as methyl donor
heterologous_genes.expression_notes: Soluble SAM-dependent O-methyltransferase. Homodimer. 4 crystal structures available for rational design. GRAVY -0.235 (hydrophilic), instability index 36.3 (stable). Note: this enzyme has dual functionality - can methylate both 2-hydroxyisoflavanone (EC 2.1.1.212) and genistein (EC 2.1.1.46). If co-expressed with IFS and without HIDH, it could act on the 2-hydroxyisoflavanone intermediate directly, but HIDH is still recommended for complete pathway.

heterologous_genes.function: NADPH--cytochrome P450 reductase; provides electrons from NADPH to both CYP450 enzymes (C4H and IFS2)
heterologous_genes.expression_notes: Essential co-expression partner for both CYP450 enzymes (C4H and IFS2). ER membrane-anchored. Requires FAD and FMN cofactors. Must be expressed at sufficient level to support electron transfer to both CYP450s. Consider 2:1 CYP450:CPR ratio for optimal electron coupling. Instability index 47.4 (potentially unstable); strong promoter and codon optimization recommended.

alternative_enzyme_candidates.notes: Alternative IFS from licorice. First characterized IFS enzyme. May have different substrate specificity profile.
Alternative C4H. Well-characterized CYP73 family member.
Alternative IOMT from licorice. Same dual EC activity (2.1.1.212 and 2.1.1.46).
Alternative CHS from alfalfa. Legume-origin may provide better metabolon compatibility with CHI/IFS.

overexpressions.rationale: Increases cytoplasmic NADPH supply. Pathway requires 2 NADPH per biochanin A (C4H and IFS steps). G6PDH2r is the primary NADPH source via pentose phosphate pathway.
Entry point to shikimate pathway. Increases flux from PEP + E4P to aromatic amino acids. Native ARO4 is feedback-inhibited by Tyr; use feedback-resistant ARO4-K229L mutant for constitutive high flux.
Channels chorismate toward prephenate (Phe/Tyr branch) rather than Trp branch. Native ARO7 is feedback-inhibited by Tyr; use feedback-resistant ARO7-T226I mutant.

additional_engineering_recommendations.rationale: Removes feedback inhibition by L-tyrosine, increasing shikimate pathway flux 3-5 fold
Removes feedback inhibition by L-tyrosine on chorismate mutase
Removes Snf1 kinase-mediated phosphorylation of acetyl-CoA carboxylase, increasing malonyl-CoA supply for CHS reaction (3 malonyl-CoA per product molecule)
Essential electron donor for both CYP450 enzymes (C4H and IFS2). Must be co-expressed at ER membrane.
Two ER membrane-bound CYP450 enzymes (C4H, IFS2) plus CPR compete for ER space. INO2 overexpression expands ER membrane area, reducing competition.

Both CYP450 enzymes (C4H, IFS2) require heme as cofactor. Native heme supply may be insufficient for two heterologous CYP450s. HEM3 (porphobilinogen deaminase) overexpression increases heme precursor flux.

native_transport.notes: Biochanin A is not a native yeast metabolite and no specific transporter exists. The hydrophobic isoflavone (logP ~2.9) may passively diffuse across membranes. For active export, consider heterologous expression of a plant ABC transporter or the yeast PDR family multidrug resistance pumps (e.g., PDR5/SNQ2). Exchange reaction added in FBA model for simulation purposes.

optimization_rounds.notes: Theoretical ceiling at 10% biomass constraint. Maximum without biomass constraint = 0.789 mmol/gDW/h.

optimization_rounds.strategy: KO ARO9/ARO8 + OE ZWF1 + OE ACC1
optimization_rounds.notes: Infeasible: ACC1 overexpression via gene-level OE forces min flux on all 13 FAS reactions (AND-logic GPR), creating impossible flux demands. ACC1 should be overexpressed via promoter replacement in vivo, not modeled as gene-level OE.
ZWF1 overexpression alone slightly reduces product flux because it forces carbon through pentose phosphate pathway. The NADPH supply is not limiting at baseline.

optimization_rounds.strategy: OE ZWF1 (min 2.0) + OE ARO4 (min 0.5) + OE ARO7 (min 0.5)
optimization_rounds.notes: ARO4/ARO7 overexpression pushes more flux through shikimate pathway toward phenylalanine. The OE slightly improves flux vs Round 2 (+1.1%). Stopped: diminishing returns. Note: the feedback-resistant ARO4-K229L and ARO7-T226I mutants recommended above cannot be modeled in FBA (which does not capture enzyme regulation) but are critical in vivo.

fba_results.strategy_description: Express 8 heterologous enzymes (PAL2, C4H, 4CL1, CHS, CHI1, IFS2, HIDH, HI4'OMT) plus CPR (ATR1) for a total of 9 heterologous genes. The pathway converts L-phenylalanine through cinnamate, coumarate, coumaroyl-CoA, naringenin chalcone, naringenin, 2-hydroxydihydrogenistein, and genistein to biochanin A. Overexpress ZWF1 (NADPH supply), ARO4-K229L (feedback-resistant DAHP synthase), and ARO7-T226I (feedback-resistant chorismate mutase) to increase phenylalanine precursor flux. Introduce ACC1-S659A/S1157A mutation to boost malonyl-CoA supply. Co-express ATR1 (CPR) for CYP450 electron supply. Consider ER membrane expansion (INO2 OE) and heme supply enhancement (HEM3 OE) for the two membrane-bound CYP450 enzymes. With best FBA strategy (10% biomass constraint), achieves 0.653 mmol/gDW/h biochanin A production. Production envelope shows approximately linear tradeoff between growth and production.

c4h_P92994.expression_risk: MEDIUM - membrane-bound CYP450, requires CPR co-expression and heme supply. N-terminal TM helix should be retained for ER targeting. Consider codon optimization for yeast.

HIDH.notes: Top interaction partner, suggesting physical association/metabolon formation

ifs2_Q9SWR5.expression_risk: HIGH - KEY bottleneck enzyme. Only 3 reviewed IFS entries in UniProt. Catalyzes unique aryl migration reaction. No experimental structure available. Consider multi-copy genomic integration (3-5 copies). The strong HIDH interaction (0.978) suggests co-localization may be beneficial - consider fusion protein or operon-like expression.

cpr_requirement.note: Two CYP450 enzymes (C4H, IFS2) compete for CPR electron supply. ATR1 (Q9SB48, 692 aa, 76.8 kDa) is ER membrane-anchored and requires FAD + FMN cofactors. Express CPR at 2-3x level relative to each CYP450 to avoid electron transfer bottleneck.
cpr_requirement.er_membrane_expansion: With 3 ER membrane proteins (C4H + IFS2 + CPR), ER membrane expansion is recommended via INO2 overexpression to prevent membrane crowding.
cpr_requirement.heme_supply: Both CYP450s require heme prosthetic group. Overexpress HEM3 (porphobilinogen deaminase) and/or supplement 5-aminolevulinic acid (5-ALA) in medium.

pal2_P45724.notes: Large homotetramer (4x 77.9 kDa) but well-expressed in heterologous systems. No cofactors needed. High Vmax = 10.5 umol/sec/mg.

4cl1_Q42524.notes: Somewhat unstable (II=49.2). Requires Mg2+ (abundant). PDB available (3TSY). Km=38 uM for coumarate is favorable.

chs_P13114.notes: Well-characterized. Multiple PDB structures. Malonyl-CoA supply is the bottleneck, not enzyme activity.

chi1_P28012.notes: Small (23.8 kDa), stable, 9 PDB structures. Fast non-rate-limiting step.

hidh_Q5NUF3.notes: Alpha/beta hydrolase fold. Well-characterized active site. Consider co-expression at high level to capture unstable 2-hydroxyisoflavanone intermediate.

hi4omt_Q29U70.notes: Stable homodimer. 4 crystal structures available. SAM-dependent - SAM supply (METAT reaction) may need boosting via SAM2 (YDR502C) overexpression.

protein_analysis.rate_limiting_step_prediction: IFS2 (CYP93C1v2) is predicted to be the rate-limiting step based on: (1) CYP450 enzymes typically have low turnover numbers (kcat < 1/s), (2) requires electron transfer from CPR, (3) ER membrane-bound limiting surface area, (4) no kinetic data available suggesting the enzyme is difficult to characterize, (5) only 3 reviewed IFS entries in UniProt across all organisms.

recommended_conditions.oxygen_level: Aerobic (default, O2 lb=-1000); required for both CYP450-catalyzed hydroxylation steps (C4H and IFS)
special_notes: Aerobic conditions are ESSENTIAL: C4H and IFS both require molecular oxygen as co-substrate
Consider phenylalanine feeding to supplement endogenous Phe supply and bypass shikimate pathway limitations
5-aminolevulinic acid (5-ALA) supplementation may boost heme biosynthesis for CYP450 cofactor supply
Galactose-inducible promoters (GAL1/10) recommended for CYP450 genes to allow two-phase fermentation: growth phase (glucose) then production phase (galactose)
Multi-copy integration of IFS2 (3-5 copies) recommended due to predicted rate-limiting CYP450 activity
ACC1-S659A/S1157A mutation critical for malonyl-CoA supply in vivo (cannot be modeled in FBA)
ARO4-K229L and ARO7-T226I feedback-resistant mutants critical for Phe supply in vivo (cannot be modeled in FBA)

known_limitations: FBA provides flux values (mmol/gDW/h) not absolute titers (g/L); actual productivity depends on biomass density and fermentation conditions
CYP450 enzyme activity, electron transfer efficiency from CPR, and ER membrane capacity cannot be quantitatively predicted
Feedback inhibition of ARO4 and ARO7 by Tyr/Phe is not captured in FBA; feedback-resistant mutants are essential in vivo
ACC1 phospho-regulation by Snf1 kinase is not modeled; ACC1-S659A/S1157A mutation is essential for malonyl-CoA supply in vivo
Product toxicity of biochanin A to yeast cells is unknown; isoflavones may inhibit growth at high concentrations
Metabolon formation between CHS/CHI/IFS (suggested by STRING interactions) is not modeled but may significantly affect in vivo flux
ER membrane surface area limitation for multiple CYP450 + CPR proteins is not captured in stoichiometric models
Heme supply adequacy for two heterologous CYP450s cannot be predicted quantitatively
The 2-hydroxyisoflavanone intermediate is chemically unstable and may spontaneously dehydrate; HIDH accelerates this but side products may form
Actual protein expression levels, codon optimization effects, and in vivo solubility cannot be predicted by FBA
Adaptive laboratory evolution (ALE) outcomes for improved growth with pathway burden are unpredictable
